This error occurs when the sender email address used in Output Parameter Determination contains a domain that has not been registered as an allowed sender domain in the system configuration. This is a security control to prevent SAP from sending emails from domains the customer does not own or has not authorised.

This error can appear in two scenarios:

  • At output runtime: when an output item is processed and the sender domain has not been configured.
  • At OPD configuration time: when a user tries to define a Sender Email in Output Parameter Determination and the domain of that address is not registered.

3.1 How to Configure Allowed Sender Domains

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define Email Sender Domain (ID: 101313)”.
  2. Enter the domain(s) that are allowed as sender domains. The following formats are supported:

Sophia.Tester@company.com (allows emails sent to this email address)
*@company.com (allows all addresses with the domain company.com)
*.company.com (allows all email addresses in the subdomain company.com, e.g. Sophia@sales.company.com)

Warning: Do NOT include the @ character when entering a domain. For example, enter company.com, not @company.com. Including @ will cause the configuration to fail silently.

Warning: The SAP domain @sap.com must NOT be used in customer systems. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2108, checks were introduced specifically to block the usage of SAP domains (sap.com or @sap.com) in the sender domain configuration.

Note: You can add multiple domains if your organisation sends from more than one domain (e.g., different domains per company code or region).

3.2 Also Define a Default Sender Email Address

As a best practice, also configure a default sender email address. This is used whenever no specific sender address is determined by the output logic. To configure it:

  1. Open the configuration activity “Define a Default Email Sender Address (ID: 101311)”.
  2. Enter a valid sender address from your registered domain, for example:

do.not.reply@company.com

Error 4: "Sender Email Address Can't Be Initial"

This error appears when the system cannot find any sender email address for the output. The error means that the Sender Email field in Output Parameter Determination for the relevant output type is either blank or has not been configured at all.

4.1 Fix: Maintain Sender Email in OPD for Payment Advice

  1. Open the Output Parameter Determination app.
  2. Set Show Rules for to, e.g.: Payment Advice.
  3. Set Determination Step to, e.g.: Email Settings.
  4. Choose the Edit button.
  5. Under Table Contents, choose + (Insert New Row) and fill in the following, e.g.:

Output type:      PAYM_ADV
Sender Email:     <your sender email address>
Email Template:   FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE

  1. Save the configuration.

Note: The email template FFO_PAYM_ADVICE_EMAIL_TEMPLATE is delivered by SAP as a standard template. You can copy and customize it to adapt the subject line and email body to your business requirements.

Tip: Make sure the sender email domain is also registered in the allowed sender domains. Using an email address whose domain is not registered will result in the 'sender domain not allowed' error even after you fill in the sender email.

Quick Reference: Error Summary and Where to Fix

Error Message

Root Cause

Where to Fix

No email recipient has been maintained

Recipient email not configured in OPD or business partner master data

OPD (Email Recipient step) and/or Maintain Business Partner

Recipient address x.y@abc.com not allowed

Receiver domain not whitelisted on test/quality system

Config Activity ID 101312

Sender domain in xxxx@xxxx.com isn't allowed / Domain in sender email address is not allowed

Sender domain not registered as an allowed domain

Config Activity ID 101313 & 101311

Sender email address can't be initial

Sender email not configured in OPD for the output type

OPD > Show Rules for: &1 > Determination Step: Email Settings > Output type

entry.title: 在SAP S/4HANA Cloud 公有云生产系统中更改会计年度变式
entry.content:
真实世界的业务是动态的。合并、收购、售卖公司或新的战略报表要求可能意味着您现有的会计年度不再满足变化的需要。

您可能会在以下情况中找到和您相同的处境：

* 场景 1：需要调整会计年度：贵公司一直在日历年（1 月 - 12 月）运营，但由于最近收购，您现在需要与会计年度从 7 月开始的新母公司保持一致。这需要创建“缩短的会计年度”，以弥合从 12 月到 7 月新会计年度开始的差距。

* 场景 2：重新定义期间：您将从标准日历月移动到零售日历，如 4-4-5 周结构。这从根本上改变了全年的过账期间定义方式。

如果您已经进入系统配置尝试进行更改，则可能会发现会计年度变式 (FYV) 字段在生产环境中不可编辑。我想解释为什么会这样，更重要的是，引导您找到正确的解决方案。

挑战：为什么我不能改动呢？

会计年度定义是财务系统中最基本的配置之一。基本上从统一日记账 (ACDOCA) 中的日记账分录到计划数据 (ACDOCP) 和资产价值的每个财务交易都与会计年度和过账期间相关联。

在没有结构化方法的情况下，在生产系统中回溯更改此定义，就像在您仍然生活在房屋中时尝试更改房屋的承重结构。这可能会导致严重的数据不一致和错误报告。由于此复杂性和跨不同应用范围的深层相关性，因此在生产系统中不允许此更改。

解决方案：由专家主导的专门项目

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版），生产系统中会计年度定义的更改将作为 SAP Data Management and Landscape Transformation (DMLT) 咨询服务提供的客户个人咨询项目进行处理。

这不仅仅是一个简单的配置切换；它是一种全面的服务，包括：

* 分析：专家将与您一起了解所需变更的全部范围和影响。

* 配置：将仔细配置新的会计年度变式。

* 数据转换：如有必要，将转换主数据和事务数据以与新结构保持一致。

* 支持：该团队将为您提供客户特定步骤的支持，以确保所有相关任务和应用程序的平稳过渡。

我们强烈建议您提前对此进行计划。在流程早期与审计员互动，并为项目留出足够的提前期。

如何开始

准备好采取下一步行动了吗？以下是官方流程：

1.发起请求：要正式请求更改会计年度定义，请使用组件 CA-LT-SRV-2CL 创建案例。

2.早期参与：如前所述，您联系 SAP DMLT 咨询服务越早，我们为您准备和计划服务就越好。

需要牢记的重要注意事项

为确保您清楚了解，以下是有关服务的几个要点：

* 系统前提条件：此服务支持通过 SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) 管理的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud（公共版）实施。

* 范围：目前，该服务涵盖跨所有分类账和公司代码分配的单个唯一会计年度变式的变更。

* 新变式标识：如果您当前正在使用预配置的 SAP 会计年度变式（例如 K4），则项目将需要创建新的客户特定变式标识（例如 Z4）。作为服务的一部分，包含旧变式标识（如表 ACDOCA 中的行项目）的数据将转换为新变式标识。

* 面向未来的变更：对会计年度定义的更改仅影响未来的数据和流程。历史交易数据的修改不在范围内。

*进一步参考 3678328 - Change of Fiscal Year Variant in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

您的财务框架至关重要，应精准、谨慎地根据不断变化的业务需求调整财务框架。虽然您无法通过单击开关简单的进行此更改，但可以使用结构化、专家主导的路径来确保其正确完成。

entry.title: PO Workflow Restart in Public Cloud – Options and Differences in Start Behaviour (Fiori vs GUI Apps)
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Purchase Orders can be approved using Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders. This approval process ensures compliance and governance in procurement processes.

In real-life business scenarios, however, it is quite common that a Purchase Order must be changed or reworked after initial approval—for example, due to price changes, quantity adjustments, or updated delivery dates.

To handle such changes correctly, SAP provides several options to control when a workflow restart should be triggered. Nevertheless, during implementation and testing, differences in restart behavior can be observed depending on whether the Purchase Order is changed via a Fiori app (Manage Purchase Orders) or via the Web GUI (Create Purchase Order) (ME22N).

This blog post summarizes the available options to control workflow restarts, highlights an observed behavioral difference, and explains the underlying cause.

Restarting Approval Workflows for Purchase Orders

 

Standard Configuration: SSCUI 103345Enhancement via BAdI: MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART

SAP provides a standard configuration node to control workflow restarts:

Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders (SSCUI 103345)

Using this configuration, you can define pre-defined conditions that determine whether a workflow must be restarted when an already approved Purchase Order is changed. Typical examples include:

  • Net Price changes
  • Quantity changes
  • Incoterm changes

This approach works well as long as the required logic fits into the predefined conditions delivered by SAP.

When additional or customer-specific business conditions need to be evaluated, SAP offers a Business Add-In:

MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART

This BAdI allows you to implement your own logic to decide whether a workflow restart is required when a Purchase Order is changed.

Typical use cases include conditional restarts:

  • Restarting workflows only for specific document types
  • Considering custom fields or business rules
  • Excluding certain changes from triggering a restart

From a functional point of view, this BAdI provides the necessary flexibility to fine-tune workflow behavior.

 

Observed Difference in Restart Behavior

During implementation of BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART, an important difference can be observed depending on the application used to change the Purchase Order:

  • Fiori App: Manage Purchase Orders
  • Web GUI App: Change Purchase Order (ME22N)

Observation

When the same Purchase Order change is performed:

  • The workflow restart behavior differs between the Fiori app and ME22N
  • The custom logic implemented in the BAdI is not always evaluated in the same way
  • As a result, a workflow may restart in one app, but not in the other

This behavior can be confusing, especially during testing, as users expect consistent workflow handling regardless of the UI used.

Root Cause

The root cause of this behavior is explained in SAP KBA Note 3736482 - Workflow restart BADI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART fiori app vs me22n - SAP for Me.

The workflow framework interprets certain changes differently depending on the UI technology

  • Different technical handling between the odata fiori and the ABAP core. 
  • When a PO change is made, at the BADI call, the 'purchaseorderitem' table is always filled with all items in a fiori call, but from ME22n the 'purchaseorderitem' table is only filled with the item being changed. 
    If a header only change is made, the 'purchaseorderitem' table will be empty in ME22n and it will contain all items from a fiori change.   

Key Insight

This is not a customizing error, but a system behavior difference described by SAP.

Summary

To summarise:

  • SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offers standard configuration (SSCUI 103345) to control workflow restarts for Purchase Orders
  • For advanced logic, BAdI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART can be used
  • When implementing this BAdI, different restart behavior may occur depending on whether changes are made via:
    • Fiori apps, or
    • Web GUI apps

When designing workflow restart logic, it is therefore important to:

  • Test scenarios using both Fiori and GUI apps
  • Be aware that identical business changes may not trigger workflows consistently across UIs

References

    • 3736482 - Workflow restart BADI MMPUR_PO_WORKFLOW_RESTART fiori app vs me22n - SAP for Me

entry.title: Integrating Business Partner from a S/4HANA Cloud Public to another S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

In integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems, Communication Management plays a critical role in securely exchanging data. This blog explains how to configure communication between two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems using standard SAP communication scenarios. The blog is co-written with @Karunaharan. 

 

Prerequisites

  • Access to the source/sender SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.
  • User must have the role, ADMINISTRATOR.
  • Target SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system details, such as Host Name, Logical System, and Business System.

 

Step 1: Create A Communication User

Use the “Maintain Communication Users” app to create a technical communication user. Make sure to provide a username and password, as both are required. Ensure that the same user is also created in the target system. This user will be used for sending and receiving data. 

1. Open the Maintain Communication Users app.

2. Choose New.

3. Enter a valid username and description.

4. Either provide a password (basic authentication) or upload certificates for certificate-based authentication.

5. Choose Create to save the new user.

OAuth or Client Certificate can be used if required. 

 

Step 2: Create A Communication System

To enable communication between two systems, you must register these systems in the Communication Systems app (that is, register the target system in the source and vice versa.

For inbound communication, your registered system calls the services provided by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For outbound communication, your registered system provides the services called by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Open the Communication Systems app.

2. Choose New

3. Provide an ID and a name for your system.

4. On the General tab, under the Technical Data section, enter the Host Name, Logical System, and Business System details of the target system.

5. In the Users for Inbound Communication tab, choose Add (+)

a. In the New Inbound Communication User dialog box, enter the username and authentication method.
(created in Step 1: Create A Communication User)

b. Click on Ok Button.

 6. In the Users for Outbound Communication tab, choose Add (+)

a. In the New Outbound Communication User dialog box, for the Authentication Method field, choose Username and Password (the same user must be added as an inbound user in the target system).

b. Click on the Create Button.

 7. Click on the Save Button.

 

Step 3: Create A Communication Arrangement

1. Open the Communication Arrangements app.

2. Choose New.

  1. Select the scenario SAP_COM_0008.
  2. Provide a name for the communication arrangement.
  3. Choose Create.

3. In the Common Data section, select the communication system
(created in Step 2: Create A Communication System)

4. Activate SOAP services using the “Service Status” checkbox.

5. Maintain Replication Model, Replication Mode, and Output Mode. More information about these properties is available subsequently. 

6. Click on the Save Button.

7. Use the “Check Connection” button to verify connectivity between systems

 

More Properties in the Outbound SOAP Service

Replication Modes

Initial Replication: Replicates all existing data from the source to the target system.

Change Replication: Only the changes made in the source system will be replicated to the target system.

 

Output Modes

Direct: Replication happens immediately after saving the changes made to the Business Partner object..

Pooled: Replication occurs periodically. In this case, only the Business Partner objects are replicated without unchanged relationships.

 

System Filter

System Filter controls the data transferred between systems. It allows only certain data to be copied or synchronized based on specific conditions.

For example:

  • Transfer only certain types of data (like sales orders or customer data).
  • Limit the data based on specific attributes (like a region or department).

 

Additional Properties in Communication Arrangement

Data Controller and Purpose

Defines the responsibility and purpose of data usage to ensure compliance with data protection regulations. The combination of data controller and purpose must be the same in both source and target systems.

 

Integration with C4C Enabled

Enables integration-specific services required for SAP C4C integration.

 

SOAP Compatibility Mode

Ensures compatibility with SOAP-based services and avoids message processing errors.

 

Conclusion

Following these steps ensures a secure and reliable integration between two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems using standard communication scenarios.

entry.title: Business Configuration related Master Data in Parallel Line
entry.content:
This blog aims to provide information about the new feature that allows copying of Master Data related to Business Configuration in a Parallel Line from the Main Line. Thanks to Divyaprakash Singh & Nicola Pace for valuable inputs.

Manage Check Runs for Business Configurations

Prior to release 2602, the "Manage Check Runs for Business Configurations" app was used to run checks for Business Configurations, displaying the progress and status of the checks. Some configuration tables are designed so that individual fields can reference business master data. For example, when configuring a production plant, a Business Configuration Expert can assign a default supplier that is automatically used when the plant receives materials. This can create a mismatch — i.e., the value stored in the configuration table may reference master data that exists in the Main Line but not in the target tenant (Parallel Line) where the configuration is being deployed. Hence, there is a need to perform these checks to ensure that there is no mismatch between the data referenced in the Business Configuration and the data existing in the tenant.

At the end of the check run, the system issues either a successful or error status:

  • Successful status means there is no missing master data — i.e., all defined references to master data in the scanned configuration tables exist in the current system where the check run was executed.
  • Error status means that some references to master data in the scanned configuration tables do not exist in the current system. Resolution options include replacing the data with master data that exists in the system or manually creating the referenced data in the system.

The recommendation is to perform the checks after executing the Branch, Rebase or Merge process — i.e., when creating a Parallel Line from the Main Line, synchronising the data from Main Line to Parallel line or vice versa.

In addition to check runs, here also possible to perform a simulation of Cross-Client Validation to check configuration consistency using the "Manage Check Runs for Business Configurations" app. This check ensures that changes in configuration data in a tenant of the development system match the configuration used by business data present in target systems, such as test or production systems. The recommendation is to always run the check before you perform a merge operation.

For further details, please refer to the following SAP Help documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/3884afb60bfa467892ba620d663620fe.html?version=2602.500

 Copy Master Data from Main Line to Parallel Line for Business Configuration via SAP for Me

As of release 2602, a new feature has been enabled that allows copying of certain Master Data from the Main Line (ML) to the Parallel Line (PL) via SAP for Me. This functionality helps maintain the consistency of Business Configurations by replacing the manual effort needed post branch. Prior to this release, customers had to manually maintain the configuration-referenced master data, the "Manage Check Runs for Business Configurations" app after the Branch process was needs to trigger and configured for the missing master data.

A flag is now available which, when enabled, allows the copy of the following Master Data objects:

  • Address Number
  • Bank Master Record
  • Business Partner
  • Cash Pool
  • Contract Account Header (Partner Specific)
  • Cost Centre
  • Customer
  • FIFM: Financing Code
  • Hierarchy Directory
  • House Bank Account IDs
  • House Banks
  • Job Code Tile (Worker)
  • JVA Agreement
  • JVA Master
  • JVA Partner
  • Material
  • Profit Centre
  • Vendor

Please note: Only during Branch process, the relevant Business Configuration master data is copied from D100 to D120 and from T100 to T120 once the flag is enabled.

It is also important to note that the feature also generates the business user in Parallel Line without any business roles assigned. There are also set of pre-requisites which needs to be met before requesting the copy of master data during branch. More information is available in the following link -

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/fe36418d4be944e6a75c8c9d9b1e482e.html

Additional Resources

  • SAP Digital Library for Parallel Line
  • Parallel Line FAQ
  • Branch Your Main Line
  • Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal 
  • Parallel Line in SAP Central Business Configuration
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, release info
  •  Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
  • Feature Scope Description
  • What’s New

SAP Community Blogs

  • Introduction to the Parallel Line
  • Parallel Line Branch via SAP for ME
  • Parallel Line as the next evolution of our Public Cloud ERP product journey
  • Rebase: Synchronize the Configurations of Your Main Line with Your Parallel Line

Thank you for reading this blog post.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @JaiSrinivasan .

entry.title: Fixing Mobile Approvals in SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Why “Open Task” Needs to Go
entry.content:
In today’s mobile‑first enterprise, executives and managers expect to approve critical business transactions anytime, anywhere—often straight from their smartphones. However, a design limitation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is creating unnecessary friction for mobile users, particularly when approving payment requests through My Inbox (SAP Fiori).

This blog explores the challenge, its real business impact, and why enabling direct access to approval details from notification emails on mobile devices is a small change that could deliver significant value.

This is exactly why the new SAP Influence idea
“Enable Direct Access to Approval Details from Notification Email on Mobile Devices in My Inbox”
(described in Enable Direct Access to Approval Details from Notification Email on Mobile Devices in My Inbox ) is so critical.

The Problem: Too Many Steps for a Simple Approval for managers including C-level leaderships

Currently, when an approver receives a notification email for a payment request and clicks the link on a mobile device, the experience is far from intuitive:

  1. The user opens the notification email
  2. Clicks the embedded link
  3. Lands only on the My Inbox task list
  4. Must locate and tap Open Task
  5. Click again to finally reach the approval details
  6. Click … to show button Approve and Submit to finally approve it

This flow may sound manageable on a desktop, but on mobile devices it quickly becomes frustrating.

Why?

  • The Open Task button is hard to find on small screens
  • Browser limitations or authorization issues can block access
  • SAP has officially confirmed that Open Task is not supported on mobile devices by design

As a result, many approvals simply don’t get completed while on the go.

Confirmed: This Is a Product Limitation, Not Configuration

SAP Support has explicitly confirmed in the case that this behavior is a design limitation of My Inbox, not a customer setup issue.

In short:

Mobile users are required to use a function that is not supported on mobile.

This leaves customers with no workaround—only delays.

Business Impact: Small UX Issue, Big Operational Risk

For finance leaders, approval delays are not just inconvenient—they can be costly.

Real‑world impact includes:

  • Delayed payment approvals
  • Slower vendor settlements
  • Reduced adoption of mobile approvals
  • Increased reliance on desktop access
  • Approval bottlenecks during travel or remote work

For roles such as Finance Managers, CFOs, CEOs, and senior executives, approvals are often time‑critical. When mobile approvals fail, they directly affect:

  • Cash flow
  • Vendor relationships
  • Operational continuity

What Users Actually Expect

Modern enterprise applications should behave intelligently based on context—especially device type.

Expected behavior is simple:

  • When a user taps an approval link from an email on mobile
  • The system should open the approval detail screen directly
  • The Open Task step should be bypassed
  • Navigation should be fast, intuitive, and reliable

This is not about changing business logic; it’s about better user experience.

A Low‑Risk, High‑Value Enhancement for SAP

From a product perspective, this enhancement offers significant upside with minimal risk.

Potential solution approaches SAP could consider:

  • Deep links that open the approval object directly
  • Context‑aware navigation that skips “Open Task” on mobile
  • Mobile‑optimized approval screens for inbox items
  • Intent‑based Fiori navigation parameters

None of these options alter approval logic or security—they only streamline access.

Why This Matters for SAP’s Cloud Strategy

This request aligns perfectly with SAP’s stated direction:

  • ✅Mobile‑first design
  • ✅SAP Fiori UX improvements
  • ✅Cloud simplicity and automation
  • ✅Executive‑ready digital experiences

By addressing this issue, SAP would:

  • Improve mobile usability of My Inbox
  • Increase mobile approval adoption
  • Reduce customer support cases
  • Strengthen SAP’s executive mobile experience

Final Thoughts: Remove the Friction, Unlock the Value

Approval workflows should enable productivity, not slow it down.

Removing the dependency on the Open Task button for mobile users would:

  • Dramatically improve user experience
  • Speed up critical financial approvals
  • Increase customer satisfaction
  • Deliver immediate, measurable business value

Sometimes, the most impactful innovations aren’t new features—they’re the removal of unnecessary steps.

It’s time to let mobile approvals be truly mobile.

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3
entry.content:
Preliminary version of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3.

You can find this preview on the current SAP Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.3 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

For any feedback you may have, try the Commenting Feature for What's New documentation in this release. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: A Missing Signal in PO Monitoring: Why “Next Delivery Status” Should Consider Order Acknowledgement
entry.content:
Transparency in procurement monitoring is essential—especially when it comes to identifying truly overdue Purchase Order (PO) items. SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud provides analytical tools for this purpose, one of them being the Fiori app Monitor Purchase Order Items.

However, a closer look at the app reveals a functional gap that can easily lead to misleading conclusions:
The “Next Delivery Status” currently considers only the Next Schedule Line Date, even when a Next Order Acknowledgement Date is maintained and lies in the future.

This blog post outlines why this behavior is problematic, what opportunity it presents, and how a small enhancement could significantly improve operational efficiency and clarity.

The Current Behavior

In the Monitor Purchase Order Items app, the field Next Delivery Status indicates whether a PO item is on track or overdue.

What is considered today:

✔️Next Schedule Line Date

What is not considered:

❌ Next Order Acknowledgement Date

This means that even if:

  • An order acknowledgement has been received
  • A future acknowledged delivery date is maintained

…the system may still mark the PO item as “Overdue”—solely because the original schedule line date is in the past.

A screenshot taken on 03.02.2025 clearly illustrates this situation: The item is flagged as overdue, despite having a valid and future order acknowledgement date.

 

Why This Is a Problem

From a business perspective, this behavior makes it impossible to reliably distinguish between two very different situations:

  1. Items that are genuinely overdue
    (no acknowledgement, no updated delivery commitment)

  2. Items that are under control
    (acknowledged by the supplier with a future delivery date)

By treating both scenarios the same, the app introduces unnecessary noise into PO monitoring.

The Opportunity

The opportunity lies in enhancing the logic behind Next Delivery Status:

When a Next Order Acknowledgement Date is maintained and lies in the future, the status should reflect this and no longer be shown as “Overdue”.

This would not replace the schedule line date—but rather complement it by acknowledging the most recent and supplier-confirmed commitment.

Expected Business Benefits

Introducing this enhancement would deliver immediate and tangible value:

1️⃣Better traceability of overdue items

Users could clearly identify which items actually require follow-up attention.

2️⃣ Greater efficiency

Purchasing teams would spend less time investigating “false overdue” items and more time acting where it truly matters.

3️⃣ Improved trust in monitoring apps

Statuses would better reflect real-world supplier communication instead of purely technical dates.

Why This Matters

The purpose of monitoring applications is not just to display data—but to support decision-making.

If acknowledged future delivery dates are ignored in status logic, the resulting indicators can unintentionally mislead users, trigger unnecessary escalations, or reduce confidence in analytical tools.

In an environment where supplier communication and flexibility are key, the system should reflect the most reliable and up-to-date information available.

Call to Action

If you have faced similar situations where PO items are flagged as overdue despite valid order acknowledgements and you believe that the Next Delivery Status should take the Next Order Acknowledgement Date into account...

👉  Please Vote to Idea # 340221 Updating of "Next Delivery Status" based on "Next Order Acknowledgement Date" on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

entry.title: Create Sales Orders - AI-Assisted Extraction
entry.content:
The Create Sales Orders – AI‑Assisted Extraction app (F8947), available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2602, enables you to transform unstructured purchase order documents—such as PDFs and scanned images—into sales orders using SAP Document AI.
By automating data extraction from incoming documents, the app significantly reduces manual data entry, minimizes errors, and accelerates sales order processing—allowing your teams to work faster and more efficiently.

What’s new compared to Create Sales Orders – Automatic Extraction

The AI‑Assisted Extraction app introduces several powerful enhancements:

  • Embedded Edition of SAP Document AI
    Uses the embedded edition instead of the basic edition for higher accuracy and richer capabilities.

  • Multi‑Language Support
    Extracts data from purchase orders in multiple languages.

  • Instant Learning
    Continuously improves extraction accuracy based on user confirmations and corrections.

  • Schema Prompts
    Supports prompts at the schema level to further enhance extraction accuracy.

Prerequisites

Before using this app, ensure that the following prerequisites are met:
  1. The integration of SAP Document AI is set up. For more information, see Integration with SAP Document AI.

  2. The Extraction of Purchase Order (SALES_ORDER_REQUEST_EXTRACTION) schema is activated in the Manage Document AI Schema Registration Subscriptions app (F8996). For more information, see PURCHASE_ORDER_STANDARD and Manage Document AI Schema Registration Subscriptions

  3. Active the feature in SAP Discovery Center Business AI Feature by searching  "Sales Order Creation from Unstructured Data", refer to the note.

 

Authorizations

To access this app, users need to have the following IAM app assigned:
  • Create Sales Orders - AI-Assisted Extraction (F8947_TRAN)

The instant learning requires another IAM app:

  • Review Extractions for Sales Order Creation - SAP Document AI(F9153_TRAN)

Usage

The basic Usage is the same as the Create Sales Orders - Automatic Extraction .

However, AI‑Assisted Extraction adds advanced learning and correction capabilities, described below.

Instant Learning

Instant Learning allows the system to improve extraction accuracy over time based on your feedback.
Prerequisites:

  1. Your user has been assigned with business catalog Sales - AI-Assisted Extraction for Sales Order Creation (SAP_SD_BC_SO_DOCUMENTAI_PC) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

  2. Your user has write access to purchase order documents in the SAP Document AI workspace. Sufficient authorization must have been assigned to your user via the Identity Authentication service on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). For more information, see Subscribing to the SAP Document AI Workspace With the Identity Authentication Service. 

     

  3. Instant learning has been activated in SAP Document AI. For more information, see Instant Learning.

When the system makes a mistake extracting information, you correct it. By confirming that fix, you’re teaching the AI how to do it right the next time. Over time, it gets smarter and more accurate, specifically for the types of documents your business uses.

If you notice an extraction result isn’t perfect, you can easily correct it and train the model for the future.

Click the "Review Extractions" button under the "SAP Document AI" menu. This will take you to the SAP Document AI workspace.

 
Here, you can correct any data that was extracted incorrectly.
 
To feed this correction back into the AI model, simply click "Confirm" on the document. Don’t worry if the status temporarily changes to "In Progress"—it will soon update to "Confirmed," and your input will be used for instant learning.

Refresh Sales Order 

This function is incredibly useful after you’ve made corrections in the Document AI workspace.

  • Clicking "Refresh Sales Order" performs a complete update.
  • Important: This action will erase most existing data in the sales order request, including any manual changes you made directly in the app.
  • It then retrieves the latest extraction result—including any corrections you just saved or confirmed—and re-processes it to determine master data and apply relevant business logic (BAdIs).

 

By leveraging these new AI-powered features, you can significantly reduce manual effort and turn your sales order creation process into a smarter, faster, and more reliable operation.

Prompts on Schema

Prerequisites:
  1. The Admin or SchemaAdmin authorization has been assigned to your user in the SAP Document AI workspace (see Subscribing to the SAP Document AI Workspace With the Identity Authentication Service).

Log in to the SAP Document AI workspace and click Schema. For details on how to access the workspace, see the help document: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/using-sap-document-ai-workspace?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

Locate the schema SALES_ORDER_REQUEST, version 1, which the app uses.

Within the schema, open the Processing Settings section.

Note: To edit the schema, click the Edit button in the section header—not the one in the page header—to avoid changing the schema version. The app is available only for version 1 of the SALES_ORDER_REQUEST schema.

For more information, see this help document: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/processing-instructions?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

A Note on Pricing

It is important to be aware that the use of SAP Document AI is a metered service. This means it is based on usage and may incur additional costs. For specific details on licensing and pricing models, please refer to the official SAP documentation or consult with your SAP account executive.

For more information, see this link: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/ai-features-for-embedded-edition

entry.title: Enhancing Credit Management with Additional Data from Credit Agencies
entry.content:
Managing credit effectively requires access to comprehensive data. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a powerful tool to help you incorporate additional credit information into your decision-making process: the BAdI (Business Add-In) for deriving values for formula parameters in credit management. Combine it with SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration to extract additional credit information from credit reports.

What Does This BAdI Do?

The BAdI UKM_FORMULA_PARAMETER_VALUE allows you to extract specific credit information from credit reports provided by credit agencies. Once extracted, you can use these values in your custom formulas for credit scoring and limit calculations. This means you're not limited to standard data fields that the credit integration solution provides. You can tailor your credit management approach to your organization's unique needs.

A Practical Example

Imagine your credit agency provides a recommended credit limit for each business partner. Using this BAdI, you can automatically extract that proposed limit and incorporate it directly into your credit limit formula. The process involves:

  • Implementing the BAdI in the Custom Logic app
  • Creating a formula parameter to receive the extracted data
  • Building a formula that uses this parameter for credit limit calculation

For instance, you might create a parameter called PROPOSED_LIMIT that pulls the agency's recommendation. Your formula could then use this value as-is or adjust it based on other factors relevant to your business.

Getting Started

You'll work primarily in three apps: Custom Logic (for BAdI implementation), Maintain Formula Parameters (to define your parameters), and Manage Formulas in Credit Management (to create your calculation logic). The SAP documentation provides detailed guidance for each step, including sample code to help you get started.

This flexibility means your credit management system can evolve alongside your business requirements, incorporating the most relevant data for informed decision-making.

Here are the relevant links to the SAP Help Portal documentation

  • Documentation of the credit integration solution:
    (Optional) Implement BAdI to Extract Additional Credit Information
  • Documentation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:
    BAdI: Derive Values for Formula Parameters in Credit Management

entry.title: Let’s Talk SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity: Landscape, Onboarding Process and Roles
entry.content:
Connecting your ERP system to multiple banks can be complex—but with SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC), organizations gain a centralized, secure, and efficient way to manage bank communications.

In this blog, we walk you through:

  • The architecture and landscape
  • The onboarding process
  • The roles and responsibilities involved in a successful integration

 

Why SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity?

 

The SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity connector acts as the central communication hub between your ERP system and banks or financial institutions.

Every message exchanged—payments, statements, etc.—flows through this connector, ensuring:

  • Full transparency of bank communication
  • Real-time updates of payment status and cash positions
  • End-to-end traceability using the Manage Bank Messages SAP Fiori app (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

Every message sent through this connector includes key details like sender and receiver IDs, message type, file name, and message content. Additionally, it contains important context information such as Swift parameters or approval user details, which are essential for seamless bank interactions.

 

System Landscape Overview

Using SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity truly enhances your control, efficiency, and transparency in financial accounting. This solution automatically updates payment statuses and cash positions in the ERP system as soon as new information is available from the banks.

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity has two main environments: test and production.

It's important to connect these to the matching systems—test-to-test and production-to-production—on the customer side. These test and production systems are kept separate for safety and further protected by different security measures.

In the SAP BTP environment, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is a managed service. This means the subaccounts won't be visible under the customer's global accounts in BTP. Still, customers can easily access the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity applications to set up and monitor their ERP connections.

There are two connections that need to be setup when you get onboarded to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity: the first from your ERP system to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, and the second from SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity to your banks or FSIs.

 

Onboarding Process – End-to-End Journey

The onboarding process is structured into several key steps to ensure a smooth and secure integration:

1. Configure Test ERP and Connect to MBC Test Tenant

Set up your ERP test system and establish the connection with the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (MBC) test tenant. Ensure that you have the required authorizations to access MBC applications.

2. Configure MBC Test Tenant and Connect to Bank

Complete the configuration of your MBC test tenant. The SAP MBC team will then establish the connection between your tenant and the respective banks or financial service institutions (FSIs).

3. Perform End-to-End Testing

Execute comprehensive end-to-end testing to validate the entire integration flow before proceeding to the production environment.

4. Configure Production ERP and Connect to MBC Production Tenant

Set up your production ERP system and integrate it with the MBC production tenant. Verify that access to all required MBC applications is properly assigned.

5. Configure MBC Production Tenant and Connect to Bank

Finalize the configuration of the production MBC tenant. The SAP MBC team will connect it to your banks or FSIs.

6. Execute Penny Testing

Perform penny testing in the production environment to confirm that transactions are successfully processed between the ERP system and the banks.

7. Production Support and Maintenance

After going live, follow standard SAP support procedures for any issues or inquiries.

For configuration changes, submit an additional request for existing connections
To onboard new banks, refer to the relevant FAQ on adding banks to an existing SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity setup

 

Roles and Responsibilities

A successful MBC implementation depends on strong collaboration between SAP and the customer.

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity Team:

  • Activated after service request submission
  • Establishes connectivity between MBC and banks
  • Resolves technical issues
  • Supports tenant troubleshooting

Customer:

The customer plays the leading role across the entire journey:

  • Provisions MBC tenants
  • Configures ERP-to-MBC authentication
  • Coordinates with banks and Swift
  • Submits service requests
  • Drives testing and validation
  • Owns contractual agreements with banks
  • Provides technical expertise internally
  • Acts as an intermediary with third-party institutions

Cloud Administrator (IT Contact):

  • Main contact for onboarding
  • Receives provisioning and onboarding emails
  • Provisions tenants
  • Creates and manages service requests
  • Assigns authorizations

Super Administrator:

  • Broader visibility across systems
  • More relevant for on-premise scenarios
  • Can also provision tenants and manage service requests

Resources:

  • SAP Help Portal: SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity 
  • Onboarding Resource Center 
  • SAP Help Portal – MBC Frequently Questions
